L1.area: User data export and socket-based request handling

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled input can override an internal parameter via object destructuring, allowing the manipulated value to be interpolated into a file path passed to a child process execution call. This enables arbitrary JavaScript file execution on the host system.

L3.area: User profile export pipeline and its socket.io entry points
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled socket data can override an internal type parameter via object spread syntax during user export operations. The unsanitized type value is then interpolated into a file path used to fork a child process, enabling arbitrary JavaScript file execution on the host system.

ground_truth.reason: The user export functionality does not validate the type parameter, and the socket handler uses object spread that allows user-controlled data to overwrite it, enabling path traversal to execute arbitrary JavaScript files via child_process.fork().
